article.title: Museeks: The Refreshingly Simple Music Player Developers Will Love
article.body_markdown:
---
title: Museeks: The Refreshingly Simple Music Player Developers Will Love
published: True
tags: music, crossplatform, tauri, react
---

## Quick Summary: 📝
Museeks is a simple, clean, and cross-platform music player built with Tauri and React. It offers essential music playback features like library management, playlists, queue control, and playback speed adjustments, aiming for a user-friendly experience across Linux, macOS, and Windows.

## Key Takeaways: 💡

* ✅ Museeks is a simple, clean, and cross-platform music player designed for local libraries.

* ✅ It focuses on core music playback features, offering a distraction-free listening experience.

* ✅ Built with modern technologies like Tauri v2 (Rust) for the backend and React.js for the UI.

* ✅ Developers can benefit from its minimalist design during work and contribute to its open-source codebase.

* ✅ Supports a wide range of audio formats and includes essential features like playlists, queue management, and a dark theme.


## Project Statistics: 📊
* ⭐ **Stars:** 2113
* 🍴 **Forks:** 227
* ❗ **Open Issues:** 57


## Tech Stack: 💻
- ✅ TypeScript


In a world where software often tries to do everything, sometimes you just want something that does one thing exceptionally well, without the bloat. For music lovers and developers alike, the search for a truly simple, elegant, and cross-platform music player can feel endless. Enter Museeks, a delightful open-source project that strips away the unnecessary, offering a pure, unadulterated music listening experience. It's the kind of project that makes you think, 'Finally, a player that just plays music.'Museeks isn't trying to be your all-in-one media center or a Spotify killer. Instead, its purpose is beautifully focused: to provide a clean, intuitive, and easy-to-use music player across Linux, macOS, and Windows. Imagine having your entire local music library at your fingertips, presented in a sleek interface that gets out of your way. It supports all the popular formats you'd expect – mp3, m4a/aac, flac, wav, ogg, 3gpp – ensuring your diverse collection is always playable.Under the hood, Museeks leverages some exciting modern web technologies. The backend is powered by Tauri v2 and Rust, offering robust performance and cross-platform capabilities, while the user interface is crafted with React.js. This combination means developers can dive into a modern, well-structured codebase if they're curious about how such a polished application is built. It’s a fantastic example of a Tauri + React app in action, making it an interesting learning resource for those exploring these frameworks.For developers, Museeks offers several compelling benefits. First, it’s a distraction-free zone for your tunes. While you're deep in thought, coding away, Museeks provides the perfect backdrop without demanding your attention with complex menus or notifications. Its features, like library auto-refresh, queue management, playlists, shuffle, loop, cover art, and playback speed control, are exactly what you need for everyday listening. Plus, the dark theme is a welcome sight for late-night coding sessions.Beyond personal use, being open-source means you can easily fork it, contribute bug fixes, suggest new features, or even help with translations, making it a great community project to be a part of. The development setup is straightforward, using `vp` (Vite+) for the frontend toolchain, making it accessible for contributors to get started quickly.Whether you're looking for a minimalist player for your daily workflow or an interesting open-source project to explore and contribute to, Museeks hits all the right notes. It’s a testament to the idea that sometimes, less truly is more, especially when it comes to enjoying your music without any fuss.

## Learn More: 🔗
[View the Project on GitHub](https://github.com/martpie/museeks)
